title: Today's Tech Brief: AMD's new pitch: our old tech is so good you should just keep using it
summary: AMD commits to AM5 through 2029, relaunching old chips; UCLA seeks pre-litigation resolution with Oracle over delayed SaaS transformation project.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories for you today, both from the world of tech. First up, AMD is taking a rather... unusual approach to selling their older technology. They've announced that they're committing to their AM5 platform through 2029, which is a pretty long-term commitment. And if that wasn't enough, they're relaunching some of their older chips, like the RX 9070 GRE, 7700 X3D, and 5800 X3D.

Next, I came across a story about UCLA and Oracle. Apparently, UCLA is seeking a pre-litigation resolution with Oracle over a delayed SaaS transformation project. It's not entirely clear what's going on here, but it sounds like there might have been some disagreements about how the project was progressing. These kinds of disputes can be costly and time-consuming, so it's likely that both parties are looking for a way to resolve the issue quickly.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: AMD’s new pitch: our old tech is so good you should just keep using it
UCLA seeks pre-litigation resolution with Oracle
